description:
Advances: specled.coverage_capture

Discovered while implementing specled_-155.5 (native/classic per-test coverage snapshot-diff engine). SpecLedEx.Coverage.Formatter takes each test's current snapshot lazily, inside its own handle_cast for the ExUnit :test_finished event, then diffs it against the previous test's boundary snapshot via SpecLedEx.Coverage.Snapshot.diff/2 to compute that test's exclusive line hits.

Root cause: ExUnit.Runner notifies formatters of test_started/test_finished via GenServer.cast (see ExUnit.EventManager.test_finished/2 and ExUnit.Runner.run_test/2 in elixir/lib/ex_unit). The Runner does NOT wait for formatters to process that cast before proceeding to spawn the next test. On a multi-scheduler BEAM, the next test's freshly-spawned process can begin executing, incrementing the same shared :cover/native counters, before this formatter's GenServer is scheduled to read them for the PREVIOUS test's snapshot. When that happens, the previous test's snapshot is contaminated with the next test's already-begun progress, and Snapshot.diff/2 (correctly, given the racy inputs) attributes the bled-through lines to the wrong test, or drops them entirely.

Measured empirically: a two-test, near-instant fixture (test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs scaffold_fixture, DefaultTest with covered.hello/covered.add) showed roughly 1-in-3 failures for exclusive per-test attribution across repeated runs with varying --seed. The race affects the native and classic snapshot engines identically -- it is about event timing, not the read mechanism.

This was descoped from specled_-155.5's per-test isolation verification item: a test asserting exclusive attribution was written, found flaky (~1/3 failure), and removed rather than shipped flaky. SpecLedEx.Coverage.Formatter's moduledoc and the specled_-155.5 amendment in .spec/decisions/specled.decision.serialized_per_test_coverage.md both carry a known-limitation disclosure pointing here.

Likely fix direction: closing this fully probably requires a synchronous per-test hook that runs inside each test's own process rather than a purely formatter-side, zero-test-code-change design -- e.g. an ExUnit.CaseTemplate-based setup/on_exit pair, since on_exit callbacks are one thing ExUnit.Runner's spawn_test/exec_on_exit synchronously waits on before advancing (unlike formatter casts). That is a materially bigger adoption/architecture change than a formatter patch.

Not currently blocking: epic specled_-155's default coverage mode is aggregate-first (no per-test attribution needed); --per-test is explicitly opt-in and already carries other caveats. Standalone, not epic-child, since it does not need to land before specled_-155 ships.
title: Per-test coverage: cross-test attribution race via async ExUnit formatter casts
